author: thegaram33
content:
Hey all, in the context of block-in-blobs (EIP-8142) it would be useful to configure the CL client to always custody the payload blobs (or enough cells to reconstruct them). This is kind of like row-wise custody, and a middle ground between a normal node and a supernode.

This would also be useful for rollup operators. But for them, the user should configure some filter, e.g. "custody blobs associated with transactions sent to address `0xabc`".

Is this feasible? <@402080271563751435> mentioned that this might already be worked on in the context of sparse blobpool / blob reconstruction. I guess the main challenge is that today's blob retrieval is column-oriented, so we'd need to introduce a new fetch-by-row primitive?
